scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff states that he is acquainted with the Defendant and based on their friendly relationship, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff for financial assistance.

6. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant as a hand loan. The said amount was transferred via NEFT bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456. A copy of the bank transaction record is annexed herewith as **Annexure A**.

7. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and the liability to repay the same through digital communications exchanged between the parties. Copies of the WhatsApp messages are annexed herewith as **Annexure B**.

8. The Plaintiff is engaged in {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} and the Defendant is engaged in {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}.

BREACH PARTICULARS

9. The Plaintiff states that the loan was advanced on the understanding that the Defendant would repay the same within a reasonable time.

10. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount by the agreed date of default, which is 20.06.2024.

11. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands for repayment. Subsequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment of the outstanding dues. A copy of the legal notice is annexed herewith as **Annexure C**.

12. Despite the receipt of the said legal notice, the Defendant has failed to repay the amount. The status of the reply to the legal notice is {{LEGAL_NOTICE_REPLY_STATUS}}.

DAMAGES

13. The Plaintiff states that due to the failure of the Defendant to repay the loan, the Plaintiff has suffered financial loss to the extent of the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/-.

14. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the outstanding amount from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realization.

15. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant continues to retain the unpaid amount unlawfully, causing continued financial loss to the Plaintiff.

LEGAL BASIS

16. The Defendant borrowed / received a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 69 read with Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

17. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

19. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

20. The breach was a single event. The Plaintiff's loss and right to claim damages continue until compensated in accordance with law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

21. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee on the plaint has been computed at Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- under the provisions of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958 as applicable in the State of Karnataka, with reference to {{COURT_FEE_COMPUTATION_BASIS}} and the applicable rate schedule. The plaint is tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

22. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Section 34 CPC;
(b) Future interest at such rate as the Court deems fit an
